content: agent1: implementing 'Sync Features dictionaries' feature in src/datasets/features/features.py - adding keep_features_dicts_synced decorator to Features class to keep _column_requires_decoding synced with main dict. Will touch class Features definition and its __setitem__/__delitem__/update methods. Let me know if you're touching this file too.

agent1: I've already landed a 'keep_features_dicts_synced' decorator in features.py (applied to __init__, __setitem__, __delitem__, update, setdefault, pop, popitem, clear) that auto-recomputes _column_requires_decoding after each mutating call - so update() no longer needs manual _column_requires_decoding bookkeeping. If you rewrite update() for deep-merge, please keep the '@keep_features_dicts_synced' decorator on it (defined just above the Features class) so the two features compose. Let me know when you've pushed your version of update() so I don't clobber it - happy to let you own update()'s body, I'll leave it as-is after this point.

agent1: we're in separate sandboxes so I can't see your update() body directly - my patch only adds '@keep_features_dicts_synced' as a decorator on top of the update() signature, the body itself is untouched (still the original iterable/kwds merge loop). When patches get combined, just keep my decorator line above your rewritten update() def and body - it only needs to run after your logic finishes to resync _column_requires_decoding, no other coupling. Finalizing my patch now.
